channel.title: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism.
channel.description: KillBait is a news aggregator developed by students and professionals in journalism and web development from Argentina, Spain, and the United Kingdom. Our focus is on providing readers with clear, concise, and relevant summaries of each news story.

item.title: Upcoming news
Study Explores How Coffee Affects Gut Microbiome, Mood, and Inflammation
item.description:
A recent study published in Nature Communications investigates how regular coffee consumption influences the gut-brain axis, revealing that its health effects extend well beyond caffeine. Researchers from APC Microbiome Ireland compared 31 habitual coffee drinkers with 31 non-drinkers and also examined the effects of temporarily stopping and then reintroducing coffee, including both caffeinated and decaffeinated versions. The findings highlight coffee as a complex dietary component that interacts with gut microbes, metabolism, and emotional well-being.
One of the key discoveries is that coffee alters the composition of the gut microbiome. Regular drinkers showed increased levels of certain bacteria, such as Eggerthella species and Cryptobacterium curtum, which are linked to digestive processes like acid secretion and bile acid production. These microbes may also help protect against harmful bacteria, suggesting a potential role in maintaining gut health.
The study also explored behav

item.title: Rising Costs Are Making Modern Dating Increasingly Inaccessible
item.description:
The article argues that while dating apps are often blamed for modern dating frustrations, the deeper issue in 2026 is economic: many people simply cannot afford to date. Surveys show that financial pressure is reshaping romantic behavior across the United States. According to recent data, 86 percent of singles have delayed or avoided dating due to money concerns. At the same time, the average cost of a date has risen sharply—about 12.5 percent in 2026—reaching roughly $189 per outing, a pace that exceeds general inflation.
This phenomenon, sometimes called “date-flation,” is hitting lower-income individuals the hardest. One-third of people earning under $50,000 report stopping dating entirely, compared to a much smaller share of higher earners. As a result, dating is becoming less spontaneous and more deliberate, often requiring budgeting and trade-offs. Financial analyst Farnoosh Torabi describes this shift as a move from organic connection to calculated decision-making, which risks

item.title: Volonaut Unveils Jet-Powered Single-Rider Airbike Capable of 200 km/h
item.description:
Polish inventor Tomasz Patan has introduced the Volonaut Airbike, a compact flying vehicle that closely resembles the iconic speeder bikes from Star Wars. Recently revealed after operating in stealth mode, the Airbike is designed as a single-rider aircraft capable of reaching speeds up to 200 km/h (124 mph). Its design stands out due to the absence of exposed propellers, instead relying on jet propulsion to achieve lift and forward motion. This gives it a sleeker and more futuristic appearance compared to many existing eVTOL (electric vertical takeoff and landing) concepts.
A key feature of the Airbike is its proprietary stabilization system, which is supported by a flight computer that enables automatic hovering and simplifies control for the rider. The riding position offers an unobstructed 360-degree field of view, enhancing both situational awareness and the immersive sensation of flight. According to Volonaut, the goal is to create a “superbike for the skies” that feels intuitive

item.title: Amazon’s New Logistics Service Pressures UPS and FedEx Shares
item.description:
Amazon’s launch of Amazon Supply Chain Services weighed on shares of major shipping and logistics companies, including UPS and FedEx, as investors reacted to the prospect of stronger competition in freight, fulfillment, and parcel delivery. The new business is designed to let companies outside Amazon’s marketplace use Amazon’s logistics network for services such as storage, shipping, and fulfillment. Market participants saw the move as an expansion of Amazon’s reach from e-commerce into a broader supply-chain platform.
The stock reaction was immediate, with transportation and delivery names falling as the market priced in a more competitive landscape. The announcement suggested Amazon is not only supporting third-party sellers on its platform, but also trying to become a direct logistics provider for a wider set of businesses. That raises concerns for incumbents whose core businesses depend on package delivery, shipping, and warehousing.
The article frames the development as part of

item.title: Could a Fight Between Kong and Godzilla Really Happen According to Physics?
item.description: In a playful analysis of the Godzilla vs. Kong debate, the article brings a scientific twist by using Galileo's law of the Square-Cube principle to demonstrate the physical limitations of these colossal creatures. According to Galileo, the larger an object grows, the greater its weight becomes compared to its surface area, making it unsustainable at enormous sizes. The article explains that Godzilla, weighing over 100,000 tons, would be unable to even move without catastrophic damage to his body due to the strain on his bones and cardiovascular system. Similarly, Kong, being a primate, would also be unable to function at such a scale due to issues like overheating and the impact force from his movements. The article concludes humorously that gravity is the true victor in any hypothetical battle between these titans, as both would be instantly incapacitated by their own size. This scientific perspective turns the epic monster fight into a tragic reality check, showing that neither creat

item.title: Eta Aquariids Meteor Shower Visible in the UK Skies
item.description: The Eta Aquariids meteor shower, a spectacular celestial event caused by the debris from Halley's Comet, is set to be visible in the skies over Britain. Occurring every year in early May, this meteor shower produces bright streaks of light as particles from the comet enter the Earth's atmosphere at high speeds. Although best observed from the southern hemisphere, enthusiasts in the UK can still catch glimpses of these meteors during peak activity, which typically happens in the pre-dawn hours. Experts recommend finding a dark, open space away from city lights to increase visibility. The Eta Aquariids can produce up to 40 meteors per hour under ideal conditions, with their luminous trails often lasting several seconds. While meteor showers are unpredictable, the Eta Aquariids are known for their consistent appearances, making them a favorite among amateur astronomers and sky watchers. The event provides a unique opportunity for photography, stargazing, and educational experiences about

item.title: Analyzing the Physics Behind Lightsaber Blades in Star Wars
item.description: On Star Wars Day, the question of whether lightsaber blades have mass is examined through the lens of physics. While characters in the movies often call them 'laser swords,' lightsabers cannot be simple lasers, since real lasers are invisible from the side, do not stop at a fixed length, and cannot interact like physical objects. By considering mass and motion, the article explains that a lightsaber’s behavior during duels suggests it has mass. Using the concept of angular motion and the moment of inertia, it demonstrates that swinging a blade with mass behaves differently than a massless light beam. The article also references the rotation of a thrown lightsaber in 'Return of the Jedi,' showing that the center of mass indicates the blade contributes to its motion. In short, lightsabers must have a physical mass component to move convincingly as seen in the movies, though the glowing blade effect is likely a visual effect. This physics-based analysis clarifies why lightsaber duels appe

item.title: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. Launches Podcast Focused on Food and Celebrity Guests, Avoiding Vaccine Topics
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr., currently serving as Secretary of Health and Human Services, recently launched a new podcast aimed at promoting 'radical transparency in government.' Despite initial expectations that the show would focus on chronic disease and controversial medical topics, the first two episodes largely center on discussions about food with celebrity guests. Reality-TV chef Robert Irvine appears in the premiere episode to discuss his efforts to improve military dining at Fort Hood, Texas, highlighting lower-sodium and fresh food options. Irvine emphasizes the role of education and smart budgeting in healthier eating but glosses over other barriers such as cost, access, and convenience. The second episode features former professional boxer Mike Tyson, who shares his experiences with growing up in food-insecure neighborhoods and the importance of mentorship for healthier lifestyles. While the podcast touches on issues like food deserts and processed foods, it provides little concr

item.title: Southern California Weather Shifts from Light Rain to Heat Wave for Mother’s Day
item.description: Southern California will experience a notable weather transition this week. Early in the week, light drizzle and cloudy, cool conditions are expected across the region, particularly in the eastern San Gabriel Mountains, Central Coast, and parts of San Diego and Orange counties. Rain totals will be minimal, generally under a quarter of an inch, with many areas seeing little to no rainfall. Monday’s highs will remain below average, with downtown Los Angeles and Burbank reaching only 67°F, while other coastal and inland areas see highs ranging from the mid-60s to low 70s. Mountainous regions may experience isolated thunderstorms and even snow in Mono County passes, while the Bay Area could see light rain and drizzle. By midweek, temperatures will rise sharply, culminating in a potential heat wave for Mother’s Day weekend. Downtown Los Angeles could reach 90°F, with neighboring valleys hitting 100°F. Inland and desert areas will face even higher temperatures, prompting moderate heat-relate

item.title: Limited-Time Discount: Bring a Colleague to TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 and Save 50% on a Second Pass
item.description: TechCrunch Disrupt 2026 is offering a limited-time promotion for attendees: buy one pass and get 50% off a second pass of the same ticket type. This offer is valid from May 4 to May 8, 2026, encouraging attendees to bring a colleague, co-founder, or partner to maximize their conference experience. Disrupt 2026, scheduled for October 13–15 in San Francisco, will feature over 300 startups, 10,000+ founders, investors, and tech leaders participating in 250+ sessions focused on innovation, funding, and growth. Attending with a partner allows participants to cover more sessions, engage in more conversations, and make strategic connections more effectively. The promotion applies to various pass types: investor, founder, attendee, non-profit, and Expo+ passes, offering savings ranging from $149 to $499. Beyond monetary savings, the offer emphasizes collaboration and learning by attending with another person, enabling deeper engagement with the conference content and networking opportunities.

item.title: Mexico's 2026-2030 Connectivity Plan: Combining 5G, Satellites, and Local Operators to Expand Internet Access
item.description: Mexico's National Connectivity Plan 2026-2030 aims to increase nationwide internet coverage from 94% to 98% by 2030, reaching over 1.4 million additional people, with special focus on vulnerable areas where digital exclusion remains a barrier to education, healthcare, and public services. The strategy emphasizes a hybrid technical approach combining terrestrial networks, satellite connectivity, and 5G expansion. Satellites play a critical role in reaching remote communities, while 5G enhances capacity, speed, and advanced use cases in urban and industrial corridors. The plan also promotes the involvement of small and local operators by reducing regulatory barriers, acknowledging that they often better understand the local terrain and deployment costs. The governance model integrates multiple stakeholders, including the Digital Transformation Agency, telecommunications regulators, CFE, PROMTEL, commercial operators, and local governments. This collaborative structure ensures connectivit

item.title: DOJ clarifies Comey indictment involves multiple matters beyond Instagram post
item.description: On May 4, 2026, the Acting Attorney General emphasized that the indictment of former FBI Director James Comey encompasses more than just a single Instagram photo, clarifying public misconceptions. The seashell photo posted by Comey had attracted widespread attention, but officials stress it represents only a small part of a broader investigation. The Department of Justice (DOJ) indicated that the charges involve multiple alleged actions and communications that are under legal scrutiny, rather than being focused on any individual social media post. CNN News Central's Kate Bolduan discussed the case in detail with criminal defense attorney Joey Jackson, explaining the legal implications and potential outcomes. While public curiosity often gravitates toward high-profile and visually striking elements like social media content, legal experts caution that such posts rarely form the core of federal indictments. The DOJ's clarification aims to ensure the public understands the scope of the ca

item.title: Minors Trespass into NYC Scientology Building, Cause Damage and Injure Staff
item.description: On Saturday afternoon, a group of teenagers forced their way into a Church of Scientology building in New York City, located near Times Square. The NYPD reported the incident occurred at around 4:24 PM when the minors entered through a side door, causing property damage. During the intrusion, a 30-year-old male caller was kicked in the leg but did not require hospitalization. The teenagers left before law enforcement arrived, and no arrests have been made. The Church of Scientology confirmed that one employee was injured and that a racial slur was directed at another individual. The disruption endangered staff, parishioners, and visitors, including attendees of a seminar. Social media videos show hooded minors rushing through the building, though authorities have not verified if these videos are directly related to Saturday’s events. This incident appears to be part of a recent viral trend known as 'Speed Runs,' encouraging minors to break into Scientology facilities. Last week, a simi

item.title: Recent Earthquakes Highlight Nevada’s Seismic Risks Near Reno and Las Vegas
item.description: Nevada has experienced a series of recent earthquakes that, while not causing major damage, underscore the state’s hidden seismic risks. On April 13, a magnitude 5.7 earthquake struck 45 miles east of Reno, shaking the small town of Silver Springs and dislodging items from shelves. Earlier, a magnitude 4.4 quake occurred north of Las Vegas, followed by a 5.2 magnitude quake east of Reno. These events highlight the many fault systems crisscrossing Nevada, including the Lake Tahoe Basin, where past major earthquakes have occurred. Experts warn that areas such as Las Vegas, surrounded by multiple faults like the Death Valley and Hurricane systems, remain vulnerable despite the lack of historic large-scale quakes. A major earthquake near Las Vegas could potentially cause billions in damages, particularly affecting older brick buildings and mobile homes. The Nevada Seismological Laboratory notes that earthquake early-warning systems, common in California, are not yet fully implemented in Ne

item.title: Scientists Trace Leonardo da Vinci’s Lineage to Reconstruct His Genetic Profile
item.description: An international scientific initiative called the Leonardo DNA Project is making strides toward uncovering the genetic makeup of Leonardo da Vinci. Spanning over 30 years, researchers have traced da Vinci's family tree across 21 generations, identifying 15 living male descendants in the direct paternal line. DNA analysis of six of these descendants has confirmed shared Y chromosome markers, validating continuity in the male lineage. Excavations at a Da Vinci family tomb in Vinci are ongoing, with recovered bone fragments undergoing radiocarbon dating and paleogenomic testing. If DNA from these remains matches living descendants, it could help reconstruct Leonardo's genetic profile. Beyond genetics, researchers are also investigating Leonardo’s family history, including his mother Caterina and grandfather Antonio, and properties once owned by the family. A newly discovered charcoal drawing in Vinci, nicknamed the 'Unicorn Dragon,' may be an early work by Leonardo, showcasing his fascina

item.title: Study Reveals How Coffee Influences Gut Health and Brain Function
item.description: Researchers at University College Cork's APC Microbiome Ireland have uncovered how coffee impacts the gut-brain axis, demonstrating that both caffeinated and decaffeinated coffee can positively shape gut bacteria and influence mental well-being. In a study with 62 participants, coffee consumption was linked to improvements in mood, reduced stress, and changes in specific gut microbes, including Eggertella sp and Cryptobacterium curtum. Decaf coffee improved learning and memory, suggesting that compounds beyond caffeine, such as polyphenols, contribute to cognitive benefits. Meanwhile, caffeinated coffee enhanced focus, alertness, and reduced anxiety. The study highlights that coffee’s effects extend beyond stimulation from caffeine, affecting gut metabolites, microbiome composition, and neurological responses. These findings suggest that moderate coffee intake, whether caffeinated or decaf, can support both digestive and mental health, potentially making coffee a valuable component of

item.title: Analyzing the Value of the Chase Sapphire Reserve Card Despite Its $795 Annual Fee
item.description: Alberto Riva, a senior editor at CNN Underscored, explains why he continues to use the Chase Sapphire Reserve® credit card despite its recent annual fee increase from $550 to $795. Riva values the card primarily for its strong travel and dining rewards, as well as its extensive travel insurance coverage. He calculates that after factoring in the card's various credits—including $300 annual travel credit, $120 Lyft credit, $300 restaurant credit, and $60 DoorDash credit—the effective annual fee for him is just $15. Additional perks like airport lounge access through Priority Pass and Chase Sapphire Lounges add further value, enhancing his travel experience. While other credits, such as luxury hotel credits, concert or streaming service credits, exist, Riva does not personally utilize them but acknowledges they may be valuable to others. He also reviews alternative cards, like the American Express Platinum, Capital One Venture X, and Chase Sapphire Preferred, noting that while they have

item.title: Concerns Raised Over Disney Owning Multiple Unused Homes in Residential Area
item.description: Access to the full news article was blocked by News Corp Australia's crawler bot detection software, preventing verification of the detailed content. Based on the headline, the article likely discusses allegations against Disney for acquiring multiple homes on a single street and leaving them unused, which critics argue could contribute to local housing shortages and escalate property prices. The story seems to focus on how such corporate real estate practices might impact the availability of affordable housing, community dynamics, and potentially local government responses to large-scale property holdings. While the exact number of homes and Disney's rationale remain unclear from the blocked content, the headline suggests a narrative of public concern regarding the effects of large companies holding significant residential property assets without active occupancy or development. The issue ties into wider debates on corporate responsibility and housing crises in urban and suburban area

item.title: Cochrane Review Finds Anti-Amyloid Drugs Offer No Meaningful Benefit for Alzheimer’s and Increase Brain Swelling Risk
item.description: A comprehensive Cochrane systematic review analyzing data from 17 clinical trials involving over 20,000 participants has concluded that anti-amyloid monoclonal antibodies, designed to clear amyloid beta plaques from the brain, provide little to no meaningful clinical benefit for patients with mild cognitive impairment or early-stage Alzheimer’s dementia. The drugs, which successfully reduce amyloid levels, fail to produce noticeable improvements in memory, cognitive function, or disease progression that would be considered relevant in everyday clinical practice. Lead author Francesco Nonino emphasized the distinction between statistical significance in trials and real-world clinical relevance for patients. Safety concerns were also prominent in the findings. The medications were associated with elevated risks of brain swelling (ARIA-E) and bleeding (ARIA-H), often detected only through imaging and not always accompanied by noticeable symptoms. The long-term implications of these side e

item.title: California's New Rules for Autonomous Vehicles: How Robotaxis Will Be Ticketed
item.description: The California Department of Motor Vehicles (DMV) recently issued new rules for autonomous vehicle (AV) testing and deployment, sparking strong reactions within the AV industry. One of the most interesting aspects of these new regulations involves how robotaxis will be held accountable for traffic violations. Under the new rules, law enforcement can issue citations to AV companies for violations committed by their vehicles, and the manufacturers must report the violation to the DMV within 72 hours. However, these citations will not involve monetary fines but will serve as data to help the DMV identify systemic issues. This rule has been met with mixed reactions from insiders, many of whom believe data collection is more valuable than financial penalties. Meanwhile, new regulations also ease the process for deploying heavy-duty autonomous vehicles, like self-driving trucks. However, the industry has expressed concerns about the burdensome requirements for data collection and sharing, es

item.title: Key stocks to watch this week: eBay, Coinbase and Palantir
item.description: The article highlights three companies that investors are watching closely in the current market environment: eBay, Coinbase and Palantir. Each of these firms operates in a distinct corner of the digital economy and is facing different sets of catalysts and risks. eBay is assessed as a mature online‑marketplace player whose stock may be influenced by shifts in consumer spending, advertising trends and competition from other e‑commerce platforms. The piece notes that eBay’s results and guidance on customer growth and margins could move the share price if they differ from what analysts expect. Coinbase, the cryptocurrency exchange, is framed as a proxy for broader crypto sentiment. The article ties its performance to expectations around trading volumes, regulatory clarity and the legal status of digital assets, especially in the U.S. Any news on enforcement actions, approval of new products or changes in crypto prices could meaningfully affect Coinbase’s valuation. Palantir, the data‑ana

item.title: Concerns Raised About Safety and Training of U.S. Truck Drivers
item.description: A trucking industry insider has raised concerns over safety risks on U.S. highways due to improperly trained commercial truck drivers, including some who are illegal immigrants. Mike Kucharski, co-owner and vice president of JKC Trucking in Illinois, emphasized that unqualified drivers present dangers to school buses, families, and commuters. Authorities have linked several fatal crashes to drivers who fraudulently obtained commercial driver’s licenses (CDLs). Notably, Ohio and Indiana reported cases where illegal immigrant drivers caused deadly accidents after securing licenses under false identities. In response, Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton and U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy have launched investigations into commercial driving schools that allegedly provide inadequate training, particularly to non-English speakers. Kucharski warns that these practices not only threaten public safety but also disrupt the trucking industry and the U.S. supply chain, increasing costs for

item.title: Top 33 Gift Ideas for Wives in 2026 Based on Interests and Lifestyle
item.description: CNN Underscored has curated a list of 33 thoughtful gift ideas for wives in 2026, covering a variety of interests from tech gadgets to self-care and home accessories. The guide emphasizes choosing gifts that align with your wife's hobbies, lifestyle, and personal preferences. Popular selections include tech accessories like the Moft Phone Tripod Stand for filming or video calls, Cozy Earth loungewear sets for comfortable weekends, and Bala Bangles for fitness enthusiasts. Beauty and self-care items such as the Therabody TheraFace Depuffing Wand, Osea Cleanse and Glow Body Set, and Dyson Supersonic Hair Dryer are also highlighted. For style and accessories, suggestions include Mejuri Duet Mixed Huggie Hoops, Quince Transit Quilted Phone Crossbody Bag, and Alex Mill’s personalized tote. The article also recommends practical household gifts, from kitchen tools like the Le Creuset Butter Crock and Ninja Creami Ice Cream Maker to planners and wellness items for better daily routines. CNN Un

item.title: SAG-AFTRA and Studios Reach Preliminary Agreement to Prevent Strikes
item.description: SAG-AFTRA, the union representing actors and other entertainment professionals, has reached a tentative agreement with major studios in an attempt to avoid potential strikes. The deal, which was negotiated after months of tense discussions, aims to address key issues such as fair compensation, working conditions, and protections in the evolving entertainment landscape. This agreement could avert labor unrest that had the potential to halt production on numerous television shows and films. Both parties expressed optimism about the potential for the deal to be finalized, though further discussions and votes are necessary before it becomes official. The situation remains fluid, with the threat of strikes still looming if the agreement is not approved. The tentative deal marks a significant step forward in labor relations within Hollywood and sets the stage for potential changes to industry standards.

item.title: Patient Experiences of Medical Neglect and Gaslighting in the Emergency Room
item.description: The article highlights the experiences of numerous patients who reported being mistreated or dismissed by emergency room staff during medical emergencies. Many shared stories of being accused of exaggerating symptoms, misdiagnosed, or ignored, sometimes with tragic consequences. Experts identify these behaviors as medical gaslighting, where healthcare providers make patients feel unheard, unimportant, or question the legitimacy of their symptoms. Vulnerable populations, including women and those facing systemic barriers in healthcare, are particularly at risk. Contributing factors can include implicit bias, inadequate communication, and lack of time or training. The article also provides practical advice on how to protect oneself from medical gaslighting, such as documenting symptoms, preparing questions, requesting second opinions, and having a supportive companion during appointments. Several cases illustrate the severe consequences of dismissive medical care, from untreated infectio

item.title: Headinvest LLC Increases Holdings in SPDR Gold Shares by 16.1%
item.description: Headinvest LLC has increased its holdings in SPDR Gold Shares (NYSEARCA:GLD) by 16.1% during the fourth quarter, adding 2,741 shares to reach a total of 19,769 shares. These shares now represent 1.6% of Headinvest LLC’s total portfolio, making GLD the firm’s 14th largest position, valued at approximately $7.84 million. Other institutional investors have also been active with GLD, including Bank of Montreal Can, which acquired a new position worth over $556 million, and Corient Private Wealth LLC, which more than doubled its holdings to over 1 million shares. Teacher Retirement System of Texas increased its stake by 103.7%, while Children’s Medical Center Corp and iA Global Asset Management Inc. entered new positions valued at $88.7 million and $77.9 million, respectively. Market sentiment for gold is mixed: weak U.S. ISM data and long-term bullish projections support demand for GLD, while rising Treasury yields, Fed policy expectations, and short-term technical resistance limit immedia

item.title: Iranian Red Crescent Submits War Crime Evidence Against US and Israel to ICC
item.description: The Iranian Red Crescent Society (IRCS) has officially submitted evidence of alleged war crimes by the United States and Israel to the International Criminal Court (ICC) and other international bodies. According to IRCS head Pir-Hossein Koulivand, the ICC has accepted these documents as official evidence, emphasizing that attacks on civilians are being pursued under the Geneva Conventions. The IRCS claims that over 132,000 civilian structures in Iran—including hospitals, schools, universities, research facilities, and bridges—have been destroyed by airstrikes. The organization shared video footage and photos to document incidents, such as the bombing of a home in Tabriz that killed four family members, leaving a 19-month-old child as the sole survivor. While Iran is not a signatory to the Rome Statute, which governs the ICC, human rights advocates suggest that Iran could grant retroactive jurisdiction, similar to Ukraine’s approach against Russian war crimes. The IRCS has formally requ

item.title: The Rise of Subliminal Audio for Self-Image and Confidence
item.description: A growing number of young women are turning to subliminal videos on TikTok and YouTube to improve their appearance and boost self-confidence. These videos often feature distorted audio affirmations, ASMR elements, or visual montages of attractive people, which creators claim can be internalized subconsciously to manifest physical changes or other life improvements. While men have historically engaged in extreme 'looksmaxxing' practices, these subliminal techniques appeal to women seeking less invasive self-enhancement. Users like Kyla and Nana report personal transformations, ranging from weight loss to improved self-esteem, and some integrate subliminals into daily routines or sleep schedules. Creators also use scientific concepts like neuroplasticity, theta frequencies, and repetition to explain potential effects, though neuroscientists caution that actual behavioral or physical changes are unlikely. Despite this, many users find them psychologically beneficial, treating subliminals

item.title: OpenAI Adds Custom AI Pets to Codex for Developer Assistance
item.description: OpenAI has introduced a new feature to its Codex app, adding customizable AI-generated pets to enhance user interaction. These animated companions do not participate in coding but serve as floating overlays, offering updates on the app's activities. The pets can notify users when Codex has completed a task or if it requires input. Developers can also create their own unique pets, which can be shared online. With built-in variations like a cat, dog, and other fun characters such as Goku and even the infamous Clippy, the feature allows for a more engaging experience. While the new pets are a lighthearted addition, some users have expressed concerns about potential security risks, especially regarding malware and misuse. Overall, the new feature aims to add personality and fun to the coding environment without compromising productivity.

item.title: Dr. Oz Leads CMS Efforts to Address Medicare and Medicaid Fraud
item.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, widely known for his television career as “America’s Doctor,” now serves as the administrator of the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) under the Trump administration. He has become a prominent figure in the administration's efforts to combat alleged fraud in Medicare, Medicaid, the Affordable Care Act, and the Children’s Health Insurance Program. Leveraging his TV experience, Oz produces high-profile social media videos documenting raids on healthcare providers and urging states to verify Medicaid provider legitimacy. He has also temporarily withheld billions in federal funds from states like Minnesota to ensure compliance. While some of his claims about the scale of fraud have been criticized as exaggerated or inaccurate, particularly in New York, certain experts and state officials agree that his actions have helped uncover real instances of abuse. Oz’s approach has been controversial: critics argue that it targets Democratic-led states for political rea

item.title: GLP-1 Medication Semaglutide Shows Association with Reduced Depression and Anxiety
item.description: A large-scale study conducted by the University of Eastern Finland, Karolinska Institutet in Sweden, and Griffith University in Australia has found that GLP-1 receptor agonists, including semaglutide (marketed as Ozempic and Wegovy), may have significant mental health benefits beyond their primary role in treating type 2 diabetes and supporting weight loss. Tracking nearly 100,000 people from 2009 to 2022, the research observed over 20,000 participants using GLP-1 drugs. Results indicate that while taking semaglutide, individuals experienced a 42% reduction in psychiatric hospital visits and sick leave, a 44% lower risk of depression, and a 38% reduction in anxiety disorders. The study also reported decreases in substance use-related hospital care and work absence by 47%, along with a lower risk of suicidal behavior. Researchers suggest that these mental health benefits could result from multiple factors, including improvements in body image, better glycemic control, reduced alcohol co

item.title: Deployment of AI Cameras in Western US Helps Detect Wildfires Early
item.description: States across the fire-prone Western United States are increasingly deploying artificial intelligence (AI) cameras to detect wildfires at an early stage. In Arizona, an AI camera in Coconino National Forest identified potential smoke from the Diamond Fire, prompting human analysts to confirm the threat and alert the local forest service and utility. Quick detection allowed firefighters to contain the fire before it spread beyond 7 acres. Arizona Public Service operates nearly 40 AI smoke-detection cameras and plans to expand to 71 by the end of summer, while the state fire agency has added seven cameras of its own. In Colorado, Xcel Energy has installed 126 AI cameras and aims to cover seven of the eight states it serves. California operates ALERTCalifornia, a network of 1,240 AI-enabled cameras. Pano AI, a technology provider combining high-definition cameras, satellite data, and AI monitoring, reported detecting 725 wildfires in the U.S. last year. The system notifies authorities abo

item.title: 12 People Injured in Shooting at Oklahoma Party
item.description: A shooting incident at a party in Oklahoma resulted in at least 12 people being wounded. Local authorities responded promptly to the scene, providing medical attention to the victims and securing the area. The circumstances surrounding the shooting, including the identity of the shooter or the motive, have not yet been publicly disclosed. Police investigations are ongoing, and officials are urging anyone with information to come forward. The incident has caused significant concern in the local community, highlighting ongoing issues of public safety and gun violence. Law enforcement continues to monitor the situation closely, and hospitals have confirmed treating multiple victims with varying degrees of injuries, some of which are serious. Community leaders are calling for calm and support for those affected while authorities work to ensure justice and prevent further incidents.

item.title: Trump Administration Fires Entire National Science Foundation Board, Sparking Anti-Science Concerns
item.description: The article discusses rising mental health issues among teenagers, attributing them partly to global challenges like climate change and potential nuclear threats, rather than solely personal factors. It highlights how some neurodiverse youth may perceive societal problems more acutely. The core focus is on the erosion of trust in scientific expertise amid overwhelming information and discomforting facts about the environment, health, and technology. The author criticizes the Trump administration for prioritizing 'alternative facts' over evidence-based science. A key event detailed is the abrupt firing of all 22 members of the National Science Board (NSB), which oversees the National Science Foundation (NSF), on April 24, 2026, without initial explanation. This move is seen as part of a broader pattern to weaken independent science policy. Potential appointments, such as non-scientist Jim O’Neill as NSF Director, linked to anti-vaccination efforts, raise alarms about politicizing scienc

item.title: NetHack 5.0 Released: Major Update to the Classic Roguelike Adds Tutorial, Lua Dungeon Generation, and Cross-Compilation Support
item.description: NetHack, the legendary roguelike computer game that has been entertaining players for 39 years, has officially released version 5.0. Developed by the dedicated Devteam, this new version brings significant updates while preserving the game's challenging and deep gameplay that fans love. Key changes include an optional tutorial for new players in the early game phases, making it more accessible for those discovering the title for the first time. The code has been modernized to compile with C99 standards and now uses Lua for dungeon generation, replacing the previous yacc and lex system. This shift is expected to make future expansions easier while keeping the build process straightforward. Cross-compilation support has also been greatly improved, allowing easier builds for various platforms. Official binaries are provided for Windows, MS-DOS, and Amiga, highlighting the game's commitment to retro compatibility, including support for classic AmigaDOS 3.0 on 68000 machines. However, as an

item.title: SNL’s Kam Patterson Jokes About Dating Megan Thee Stallion on Weekend Update
item.description: On the May 3, 2026 episode of Saturday Night Live, comedian Kam Patterson made headlines during the 'Weekend Update' segment when he humorously commented on Megan Thee Stallion's recent breakup with Klay Thompson. Patterson playfully suggested that the rapper needed a real man, specifically one who is employed in the entertainment industry, potentially on a sketch show. As the joke developed, he revealed that his description of the ideal man actually matched himself, though he acknowledged in a self-deprecating manner that he might not be the man she wants or needs. The segment captured attention online, as fans and viewers shared reactions to Patterson's humorous and awkward attempt at flirting with Megan Thee Stallion on national television. The sketch highlighted both Patterson's comedic style and the ongoing public fascination with celebrity relationships. While the comments were made in jest, the moment became a talking point on social media, illustrating how late-night comedy oft

item.title: Iowa woman dies after post-surgery complications reportedly overlooked by hospital staff
item.description: Laura Belt, a 46-year-old woman from Iowa, died in May 2024 due to complications following a routine hernia repair surgery. According to a lawsuit filed by her family, nurses at Decatur County Hospital dismissed her post-surgery symptoms, including the drainage of fecal matter from her incision and a lack of bowel movements. Despite her distress and attempts to communicate with the nurses, she was reassured that the drainage was normal. The situation escalated, and she was later diagnosed with sepsis and necrotic tissue at another hospital. Belt's family is suing Dr. Edwin Vincent Wehling, who performed the surgery, along with nurses Brandi Oesch and Tammy Roberts, alleging medical malpractice. The Iowa Board of Medicine has also charged Dr. Wehling with professional incompetency. The lawsuit claims that necessary antibiotics were never properly prescribed, and the family argues that earlier intervention could have prevented her death. A trial is scheduled for August 23, 2026. The accu

item.title: Wall Street Journal Highlights Risks of Subsidized AI Revenue and Investor Transparency
item.description: A Wall Street Journal opinion piece raises concerns about the reliability of reported AI company revenues. Some AI firms are reportedly paying partners or subsidizing clients to use their products, which could distort the appearance of genuine demand. Examples cited include OpenAI's $1.5 billion joint venture with private-equity firms, Anthropic's $200 million contribution to a private-equity partnership, and Google's $750 million support for Gemini's adoption by consulting firms. Robert Pozen, an experienced investor and MIT lecturer, warns that these financial arrangements may lead to misleading sales figures. He notes that customers or portfolio companies might adopt AI tools because of financial incentives rather than genuine need, potentially causing revenues to drop when subsidies end. Pozen urges investors to examine what portion of AI sales is driven by such arrangements, along with retention rates of unsubsidized clients. The article emphasizes a lack of transparency in AI fin

item.title: TDK Ventures’ Nicolas Sauvage focuses on AI infrastructure, robotics, and future bottlenecks
item.description:
Nicolas Sauvage, founder of TDK Ventures, is building an investment strategy around what he calls the less glamorous but critical layers of artificial intelligence. Speaking at a StrictlyVC event, Sauvage explained his belief that the most valuable opportunities become obvious only after about four years, which has guided his firm’s approach since its launch in 2019. Managing roughly $500 million across four funds, TDK Ventures has focused on identifying future bottlenecks in emerging technologies before they become widely recognized.
One prominent example is Groq, an AI chip company focused on inference—the computational work required when AI systems respond to queries. Sauvage invested early, before the generative AI boom made such infrastructure investments more obvious. He saw long-term demand growing due to increasing AI usage, especially as AI agents now perform complex, multi-step tasks requiring repeated computations.
Beyond chips, Sauvage is investing in physical AI, particu

item.title: Jonathan Greenberger Assumes Role as POLITICO Global Editor-in-Chief
item.description:
Effective May 1, 2026, Jonathan Greenberger has officially assumed the position of Global Editor-in-Chief at POLITICO. Greenberger, who joined the organization in 2024 as an Executive Vice President, succeeds POLITICO co-founder John Harris. Harris, having led the publication for nearly two decades, is transitioning into the role of company chairman. The appointment follows an extensive search process conducted by the publication to identify a leader capable of balancing journalistic excellence with business development and platform innovation.
Before his tenure at POLITICO, Greenberger built a significant reputation within the media industry, most notably serving for nearly a decade as the Washington Bureau Chief for ABC News. During that period, he oversaw major political coverage, including presidential elections, impeachments, and the January 6 Capitol attack, in addition to managing the Sunday morning program 'This Week with George Stephanopoulos.' Throughout his time at POLITICO

item.title: Economist Warns U.S. Gas Prices Could Remain High for Years Due to Global Supply Disruptions
item.description: Economist Henrietta Treyz, co-founder of Veda Partners and director of economic policy, provided a sobering forecast about the future of gas prices during a segment on MS NOW. When asked by anchor Alex Witt how long it would take for gas prices to decrease if a hypothetical end to Donald Trump's Iran war occurred, Treyz explained that supply chain issues would prevent a quick drop. She highlighted that tanker flows through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical route for one-fifth of the world’s oil, might not stabilize until September. Treyz also noted the dramatic 300% increase in tanker insurance costs and the destruction of 80 major refining and energy facilities, which will take years to rebuild. Based on current trends, she projected gas prices could reach $4.90 per gallon the following week and potentially surpass $5 by Memorial Day. Treyz emphasized that even after normal supply resumes, it could take years before prices drop to the levels Americans are accustomed to seeing before th

item.title: US Begins Operations to Facilitate Safe Passage of Ships Through Strait of Hormuz Amid Tensions with Iran
item.description: The United States has announced that it will begin guiding merchant ships through the Strait of Hormuz, a critical chokepoint for global oil and gas trade, amid ongoing tensions with Iran. The US stresses that this initiative, called Project Freedom, is not an escort mission but aims to ensure safe passage for commercial vessels stranded due to the conflict. CENTCOM has stated that the operation will involve guided-missile destroyers, more than 100 land- and sea-based aircraft, multi-domain unmanned platforms, and roughly 15,000 service members. Iran has strongly warned that any foreign military presence in the strait will be met with force, viewing the US actions as a potential violation of the ceasefire. Separately, an Iranian vessel previously seized by US forces, the M/V Touska, has been returned, with its crew repatriated through Pakistan in a move described as a confidence-building measure. The situation has left approximately 20,000 seafarers stranded, with shortages of food, wa

item.title: Poll Shows Rising Gas Prices Leading Republicans to Blame Trump
item.description: CNN's chief data analyst Harry Enten highlighted a significant polling trend showing that even Donald Trump's most loyal Republican supporters are increasingly blaming him for rising gas prices linked to the ongoing Iran war. According to the poll, 77% of Americans overall hold Trump responsible for the increase in fuel costs, a level of blame higher than that historically assigned to former presidents Bush, Obama, or Biden. Notably, this backlash extends into Trump's own party, with 55% of Republican respondents expressing that he is accountable for the spike. Enten emphasized that this is unprecedented, as traditionally a president's core supporters are less likely to assign direct blame. The poll also indicates that a majority of Americans, 64%, do not believe that the Iran conflict justifies the higher gas prices, suggesting a broad public disapproval of the economic consequences tied to the military action. This data reflects a rare convergence of opinion among Democrats, independ

item.title: Roblox Reports Slower Growth Following Mandatory Age-Verification Implementation
item.description: Roblox has revised its full-year 2026 bookings forecast downward by approximately $900 million, citing stronger-than-expected headwinds from the mandatory age-verification rollout. Introduced in January, the age-verification system requires users to verify their age to access chat features, affecting a user base largely composed of children and teenagers. As of the end of Q1, 51% of global daily active users and 65% of U.S. users completed age verification. This initiative has contributed to slower user acquisition and engagement, with daily active users reaching 132 million, below the forecasted 143.8 million, and hours engaged totaling 31 billion, compared to the expected 33.68 billion. Additional challenges include Russia's platform block since December 2025. The safety push has also resulted in legal costs, with Roblox accruing $57 million in Q1 for settlements related to youth consumer protection and digital safety. Despite the short-term financial impact, Roblox emphasizes that t

item.title: Redondo Beach Pier Temporarily Evacuated During BeachLife Festival; Reopens Safely
item.description: On Sunday, the Redondo Beach Pier was temporarily evacuated due to police activity, prompting a delay in the BeachLife Festival scheduled for its final day. Authorities issued the evacuation around 11 a.m. through the city's alert system, advising the public to leave the pier area and closing nearby streets, including Catalina Avenue between Torrance Boulevard and Beryl Street. By 11:57 a.m., police confirmed the pier would remain closed until further notice. After thorough checks of the pier and surrounding festival grounds, officials declared the area safe at 2:21 p.m., allowing the pier and the festival to reopen. City Councilmember Brad Waller explained that a potential threat led to the evacuation, and police, along with K-9 units, conducted a careful sweep. No threat was found, and festival organizers were cleared to open to attendees. The BeachLife Festival, featuring artists such as My Morning Jacket, Peach Pit, Sheryl Crow, and James Taylor, experienced a delay of approximatel

item.title: Multiple People Injured in Shooting at Arcadia Lake Near Oklahoma City
item.description: A shooting occurred at a party near Arcadia Lake, close to Oklahoma City, late on the evening of May 3, 2026, leaving at least ten people hospitalized. Police in Edmond, Oklahoma, reported that they received multiple calls about gunfire around 9 p.m. during a gathering of young people. While ten victims were transported to nearby hospitals, authorities indicated that additional victims may have left the scene and driven themselves to medical facilities. Officials stated that the injured individuals are in various conditions, though no specific details about their injuries or the identities of the victims have been released. Law enforcement is investigating the incident, but no suspects or motives have been disclosed as of the latest reports. The event highlights ongoing concerns about public safety at social gatherings in the region. Authorities are urging anyone with information to come forward to assist in the investigation. As of now, the situation remains under investigation, and u

item.title: Harvard study finds AI models can match or exceed internal medicine doctors in ER diagnosis accuracy
item.description: A recent Harvard Medical School study evaluated how OpenAI's large language models perform in medical diagnostics, specifically in emergency room settings. The study compared the performance of two internal medicine attending physicians to two AI models, o1 and 4o, using 76 patient cases from Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center. Independent physicians assessed the accuracy of the diagnoses without knowing their source. The findings showed that the o1 model achieved the exact or nearly correct diagnosis in 67% of initial ER triage cases, outperforming the physicians who scored 55% and 50%. The study emphasized that the AI was provided the same text-based patient information as the doctors, without pre-processing. Researchers caution that while AI shows potential, it is not yet ready to replace human decision-making in life-or-death ER situations. The study calls for further prospective trials in real-world clinical environments. Experts also highlighted that comparing AI to internal me

item.title: Armed Suspect Holds Hostage Following Beverly Hills Police Chase
item.description: On Sunday evening, a police pursuit in Beverly Hills escalated into a prolonged standoff after a suspect, wanted for allegedly attempting to murder a Los Angeles County sheriff’s deputy, fled in a pickup truck and crashed into another vehicle. The incident began early Sunday morning in Lennox, near Inglewood, when law enforcement tried to stop the suspect at the intersection of Hawthorne Boulevard and West 104th Street. During the stop, a struggle occurred, and the suspect struck a deputy with his vehicle, though the deputy was not seriously injured. Later in the afternoon, Beverly Hills police located the suspect’s gray Ford pickup via an automated license plate recognition alert and attempted a traffic stop, which led to the chase ending in a collision at the busy intersection of Robertson Boulevard and Burton Way, near the Beverly Center and Cedars-Sinai Medical Center. As of 9 p.m., the suspect was holding a hostage inside the vehicle, prompting a Los Angeles County SWAT team to en

item.title: Evidence-Based Benefits of Magnesium and Creatine During Menopause
item.description: Menopause can bring a variety of challenging symptoms, including hot flashes, night sweats, sleep disruption, anxiety, brain fog, and changes in muscle and bone health due to declining estrogen levels. While hormone replacement therapy (HRT) is often the most effective treatment, not everyone can or wants to use it, which is why many turn to dietary supplements. Current evidence highlights magnesium and creatine as two supplements with the most potential benefits for women during perimenopause and menopause. Magnesium supports over 300 metabolic processes and may improve sleep, modestly reduce anxiety, and help maintain bone density, although it does not affect hot flashes or cognitive symptoms. Creatine, traditionally studied in men, has shown promising results for perimenopausal women by enhancing muscle strength, improving sleep quality, and possibly supporting mood and cognitive function. Other popular supplements, such as lion’s mane and collagen, have limited or mixed evidence, a

item.title: Zacks Research Projects Q1 2026 Earnings for Diamondback Energy (FANG)
item.description: Zacks Research has issued its Q1 2026 earnings estimate for Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ:FANG), forecasting the oil and natural gas company to earn $3.50 per share for the quarter. The firm also maintains a “Strong-Buy” rating on the stock. Analysts project the company's full-year 2026 earnings at $18.84 per share, with quarterly estimates of $4.57 for Q2, $4.47 for Q3, and $4.35 for Q4. Looking ahead, estimates for 2027 and Q1 2028 suggest continued strong performance. Other analysts have weighed in as well, with price targets ranging from $222 to $245 and ratings mostly in the “Buy” or “Strong-Buy” range. Diamondback Energy’s stock recently opened at $207.65, with a market capitalization of $58.41 billion and a PE ratio of 37.01. The company’s revenue fell 9% year-over-year in the last quarter, reporting $3.38 billion, with EPS of $1.74, below consensus expectations. Diamondback increased its quarterly dividend to $1.05, representing a 2% yield. Insider sales have occurred, but i

item.title: Starting Line-Ups for Aston Villa vs Tottenham Hotspur Premier League Match
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur has confirmed its starting line-up for tonight’s Premier League match against Aston Villa, scheduled for 7 pm UK time. Head coach Roberto De Zerbi has made four changes from the team that beat Wolves 1-0 last Saturday. Due to injuries to Xavi Simons and Dominic Solanke, Mathys Tel and Richarlison are included in the starting XI. Destiny Udogie returns to replace Djed Spence, while Joao Palhinha comes in for Yves Bissouma in midfield following his match-winning performance last week. Pape Matar Sarr, who has been out for the last two games due to injury, is included on the substitutes bench. Tottenham’s starting eleven features Kinsky in goal, with a defense led by Danso, Palhinha, and van de Ven as captain, supported by Udogie, Gallagher, Pedro Porro, Bentancur, Richarlison, Tel, and Kolo Muani. Substitutes include Austin, Dragusin, Bissouma, Maddison, Gray, Bergvall, Spence, Sarr, and Souza. Aston Villa’s lineup includes Martinez in goal, with a defense of Cash, Lind

item.title: GameStop Proposes $56 Billion Acquisition of eBay with Potential Hostile Approach
item.description: On May 3, 2026, GameStop proposed to acquire eBay in a $56 billion cash-and-stock deal, offering $125 per share in a 50-50 mix of cash and GameStop stock. CEO Ryan Cohen stated that he is prepared to pursue the offer directly with eBay shareholders if the board rejects it. The proposal is notable because eBay's market capitalization is nearly four times larger than GameStop's, making it an ambitious move for the video game retailer. GameStop has already built a 5% stake in eBay and plans to leverage its 1,600 U.S. locations to enhance eBay's authentication, fulfillment, and live commerce operations. Cohen projects $2 billion in annual cost savings within a year, aiming to boost eBay's earnings per share. He intends to serve as CEO of the combined company if the acquisition succeeds. While GameStop is working to expand its market value following a history of volatility and the meme-stock phenomenon of 2021, eBay continues to show growth, driven by collectibles, motor accessories, and li

item.title: TikToker launches campaign for public-backed Spirit Airlines revival after sudden shutdown
item.description: Over the weekend, Spirit Airlines abruptly ceased operations, canceling all flights and laying off 17,000 employees, leaving passengers confused and stranded. The airline, known for its low fares, sparked an unusual response from social media. Hunter Peterson, a voice actor frustrated with frequent flying experiences, posted a TikTok suggesting that Americans could collectively buy the airline by contributing the equivalent of a Spirit fare. Termed “Spirit 2.0: Owned by the People,” the campaign quickly gained attention. Within hours, a website was created, and by the next day, 36,000 individuals had pledged nearly $23 million. However, these pledges are non-binding, and the actual cost of purchasing and relaunching an airline is in the billions. Peterson has openly acknowledged the humorous and symbolic nature of the effort while inviting professionals like aviation lawyers and PR experts to join him. The viral campaign highlights both public fascination with airline ownership and the

item.title: Rudy Giuliani Hospitalized in Critical but Stable Condition
item.description: Former New York Mayor Rudy Giuliani, widely known as 'America's Mayor' for his leadership during the 9/11 attacks, has been hospitalized and is in critical but stable condition, according to his spokesperson Ted Goodman. Giuliani, 81, has not disclosed the reason for his hospitalization. In recent public appearances on his X show “America’s Mayor Live,” he mentioned having a hoarse voice and was seen coughing. Despite occasional missed broadcasts, Giuliani has remained active in media work through LindellTV. Former Mayor Eric Adams extended his best wishes, emphasizing the importance of transcending politics during such personal health challenges. Former President Donald Trump also expressed support on Truth Social, highlighting Giuliani's past leadership and framing his current condition as a consequence of political opposition. Giuliani has faced multiple legal challenges, including state criminal charges linked to election subversion in Arizona and a large defamation judgment in Geo

item.title: Study Suggests Quantum Collapse Could Impose Limits on Time Precision
item.description: Recent research by an international team of physicists, supported by the Foundational Questions Institute (FQXi), investigates how quantum collapse models could subtly affect time itself. Quantum mechanics allows particles to exist in multiple states at once, described mathematically by a wavefunction. Normally, these superpositions collapse into a single outcome when observed. However, alternative theories known as spontaneous quantum collapse models suggest wavefunctions might collapse independently of observation, potentially influenced by gravity. The researchers studied two prominent models: the Diósi-Penrose model, which links gravity to wavefunction collapse, and Continuous Spontaneous Localization, connecting quantum behavior to fluctuations in spacetime. Their findings indicate that if these models are accurate, time cannot be perfectly precise but contains an inherent, extremely small uncertainty. This imposes a theoretical limit on clock accuracy, although current atomic clo

item.title: United Airlines 767 Hits Truck and Streetlight During Newark Landing; No Passengers Injured
item.description: A United Airlines Boeing 767 traveling from Venice, Italy, collided with a truck and a highway streetlight while landing at Newark Liberty International Airport on May 3, 2026. The incident occurred near Runway 29, which begins less than 400 feet from the New Jersey Turnpike, where the plane's landing gear and underside struck a pole and a tractor-trailer. The pole subsequently hit a nearby Jeep. The truck driver, Warren Boardley, sustained non-life-threatening cuts from broken glass and was treated at a hospital. Eyewitnesses reported seeing debris and feeling strong gusts of wind as the low-flying plane passed. Despite the collision, all 200 passengers and 10 crew members on board were unharmed, and the aircraft taxied safely to the gate. United Airlines stated that its maintenance team is evaluating the plane's damage and that a thorough safety investigation is underway. The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) is sending an investigator to analyze the cockpit voice recorder

item.title: Rudy Giuliani hospitalized in critical condition, according to Trump
item.description: Rudy Giuliani, former Mayor of New York City and former personal lawyer to Donald Trump, has reportedly been hospitalized and is in critical condition. According to statements made by Donald Trump, Giuliani's health situation has become serious, though detailed medical information has not been publicly disclosed. This development has raised concerns among political circles and supporters, given Giuliani's prominence in recent political events and his legal involvement with former President Trump. Hospitals and officials have not yet released formal statements, and the circumstances surrounding his hospitalization remain largely private. The news has been widely reported by multiple media outlets, emphasizing the gravity of the situation and the potential implications for those close to him and involved in ongoing political matters. Public reaction has been swift, reflecting both concern for his health and the broader interest in the political implications of such developments.

item.title: Sen. Fetterman Criticizes Socialist Influence in Democratic Party
item.description: During a recent appearance on Fox News, Pennsylvania Senator John Fetterman expressed concern over what he perceives as a growing socialist trend within the Democratic Party. Fetterman discussed the May Day protests across the country, criticizing Fox News for highlighting a small number of communist symbols and calling it a misleading representation of the broader movement. He argued that the nomination of Senate candidate Graham Platner in Maine, along with pro-Palestinian activism and critiques of Immigration and Customs Enforcement practices, reflects a shift toward socialism in the party. Fetterman has frequently taken positions that differ from mainstream Democrats, including supporting the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, voting to advance the nomination of Markwayne Mullin for Homeland Security Secretary, and opposing efforts to block a potential U.S. invasion of Cuba. Despite his dissenting positions, he described his relationship with other Democrats as cordial, noting that he may n

item.title: Yankees send Anthony Volpe to Triple-A as Jose Caballero solidifies shortstop role
item.description: The New York Yankees have made a roster adjustment at the shortstop position following the end of Anthony Volpe's rehab assignment. Volpe, recovering from left shoulder surgery, hit .250 across 13 games with the Double-A Somerset Patriots but will not rejoin the Yankees immediately. Instead, he has been optioned to Triple-A Scranton/Wilkes-Barre to continue regaining form. This move comes as Jose Caballero, who was acquired last season, has been performing strongly at shortstop, providing both defensive stability and timely hitting for the team. Caballero has started 34 games this season, batting .259 with four home runs and 12 RBIs, earning praise from manager Aaron Boone for his consistency and smooth fielding. Volpe, a 2019 first-round pick, had a challenging 2025 season, struggling with injuries that included two cortisone shots, and finishing with 19 errors, the third-highest among MLB shortstops. The Yankees are not ruling out Volpe returning later in the season but are prioritiz

item.title: Spirit Airlines Shuts Down Amid Financial Struggles and Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: Spirit Airlines ceased operations on Saturday, citing skyrocketing fuel prices influenced by geopolitical tensions, including President Trump's military actions in Iran. However, top White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett criticized the airline, arguing that Spirit's repeated Chapter 11 filings and flawed business model were the real reasons for its collapse. Hassett noted that other airlines have managed to mitigate fuel cost spikes through strategic hedging. The airline's financial troubles were compounded by the cancellation of a merger with JetBlue, which Hassett attributed to the Biden administration. Despite discussions of a last-minute $500 million rescue plan during the Trump administration, efforts to save Spirit Airlines failed. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy stated that the airline was struggling even before the Iran conflict and that Trump had attempted, unsuccessfully, to keep it afloat. Overall, the closure highlights how a combination of long-standing financial

item.title: 'Dutton Ranch' Trailer Released: Beth and Rip Dutton Return in Yellowstone Spinoff
item.description: Fans of the hit series 'Yellowstone' are eagerly anticipating the spinoff 'Dutton Ranch,' following the return of Beth Dutton and Rip Wheeler. The new series, premiering on Paramount+ on May 15, explores Beth and Rip’s new life in Texas after leaving Montana, as they face new conflicts and power struggles. The trailer teases intense drama, including violent confrontations, fire, and mysterious threats, while also introducing new characters played by Annette Bening and Ed Harris. Key returning characters include Finn Little as Carter, the adopted son of Beth and Rip. The storyline highlights the couple navigating a harsh Texas landscape while preserving the legacy of the Dutton family. Fans have expressed excitement on social media, praising the return of the central couple and speculating about the spinoff's plot developments. Kelly Reilly, who plays Beth, explained that the series will show a more mature version of her character, maintaining her strength and complexity while evolving

item.title: Three People Die in Suspected Hantavirus Outbreak on Cruise Ship
item.description: A suspected outbreak of hantavirus aboard the Dutch-flagged cruise ship MV Hondius in the Atlantic Ocean has resulted in three deaths and at least three illnesses, according to the World Health Organization (WHO) and South Africa’s Department of Health. The victims include an elderly couple and a British passenger who became seriously ill and is currently in intensive care in Johannesburg. Two other crew members remain on the ship with severe symptoms and are awaiting medical transfer in Cape Verde. The cruise, which departed Argentina about three weeks ago, included stops in Antarctica, the Falkland Islands, Saint Helena, and Ascension Island, with around 150 passengers and 70 crew members on board. Hantaviruses, mainly transmitted through rodent urine or feces, can cause hantavirus pulmonary syndrome or hemorrhagic fever with renal syndrome, both severe illnesses. While person-to-person transmission is rare, WHO is conducting further laboratory and epidemiological investigations, inc

item.title: David Benavidez dominates Zurdo Ramirez to become three-division world champion with a sixth-round knockout
item.description: David Benavidez put on a dominant performance against Gilberto 'Zurdo' Ramirez, securing a sixth-round knockout to claim the WBA and WBO cruiserweight titles. With this victory, Benavidez becomes a three-division world champion, holding titles at super middleweight, light heavyweight, and cruiserweight. Ramirez, who had no answer to Benavidez's speed and power, was dropped twice in the fight, with the second knockdown in Round 6 leading to his decision to remain on the canvas. Following his victory, Benavidez called out Canelo Alvarez, although a fight between the two seems unlikely given their respective careers. The win also highlights Benavidez’s ability to transition smoothly into higher weight classes, as his power and precision translated effectively into the cruiserweight division. Benavidez's win marks a major moment in his career, confirming his status as one of boxing’s top pound-for-pound fighters.

item.title: Passenger detained after attacking flight attendant and attempting to enter cockpit on United Airlines flight
item.description: A 48-year-old man was detained after allegedly attacking a flight attendant and attempting to access the cockpit aboard United Airlines Flight 1837 as it landed at Newark Liberty International Airport on Saturday, May 2, 2026. The plane, which took off from the Dominican Republic, had 170 passengers and six crew members aboard. Audio from air traffic control revealed the pilot declaring an emergency after the suspect's violent behavior, including an attempt to open the forward cabin door. Authorities confirmed that the man was taken to a hospital for psychiatric evaluation. No injuries were reported, although one person on board refused medical attention. The Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) is investigating the incident, which is part of a troubling trend of unruly passenger behavior. According to the FAA, nearly 500 such incidents have been reported so far in 2026. Passengers who engage in violent or disruptive behavior could face fines of up to $43,658. The airline expressed gr

item.title: Rosyth councillor calls for public input on plans to host Trident submarines
item.description:
A local councillor in Rosyth, Scotland, has criticized the UK Government for refusing to hold a public consultation بشأن plans to bring Trident nuclear submarines to the Rosyth Dockyard. Councillor Brian Goodall of the Scottish National Party (SNP) described the decision as undemocratic, arguing that residents should have a say in a proposal that could significantly affect their safety and community.
The controversy centers on plans to temporarily station next-generation Dreadnought-class submarines at Rosyth while facilities at Faslane are upgraded. The UK Government has allocated £340 million to support this interim arrangement, which is expected to last until the mid-2030s. Although the submarines are primarily maintained elsewhere, Rosyth would serve as a temporary base during this transitional period.
Goodall raised concerns about the potential risks associated with nuclear-powered—and possibly nuclear-armed—submarines docking in the area. He claimed that such a move could incre

item.title: Spirit Airlines Ceases Operations Following Unsuccessful Bailout Negotiations
item.description: Spirit Airlines announced on May 2, 2026, that it is ceasing all operations after failing to secure a $500 million federal bailout. The airline's parent company, Spirit Aviation Holdings, confirmed the start of an orderly wind-down, cancelling all flights and promising automatic refunds for ticket holders who purchased directly through Spirit. Travelers who booked through third parties are advised to contact their point of purchase. The shutdown follows financial struggles that included two bankruptcy filings since 2024, job cuts, and route reductions. Rising fuel costs exacerbated by the Iran war significantly affected the airline's finances, though the Department of Transportation noted that Spirit was already in a precarious position before these external factors. Efforts by the Trump administration to rescue the airline failed due to creditor opposition, despite proposals offering a 90% government stake. Spirit, known for its low-cost, 'no frills' approach and bright yellow planes,

item.title: Cameron Young Wins Cadillac Championship at Trump National Doral, Meets President Trump
item.description: Cameron Young has emerged as one of golf's hottest players in 2026, following an impressive streak of performances on the PGA Tour. After strong finishes at the Genesis Invitational and Bay Hill, Young captured a victory at THE PLAYERS Championship and later tied for third at The Masters. His momentum continued at the Cadillac Championship held at Trump National Doral, where he achieved a dominant six-shot victory over Scottie Scheffler, claiming a $3.6 million prize. The tournament was notable for President Donald Trump's attendance, which affected security and tournament procedures. Young described the experience as unique and expressed gratitude for the opportunity to compete in front of the president, calling it an honor. He emphasized the appreciation and support Trump has for golf and thanked him and his organization for hosting such a prestigious event at the Blue Monster course. Young now looks ahead to his next challenge at the PGA Championship at Aronimik, aiming for his firs

item.title: US Pledges $100 Million for Repairs of Chernobyl Nuclear Containment Shelter
item.description: The United States has announced a contribution of $100 million to aid in repairing the damaged containment dome at the Chernobyl nuclear plant in northern Ukraine. The plant, known for the catastrophic reactor explosion in 1986, still houses significant radioactive materials within its shelter, designed to prevent environmental contamination. The funding is part of a larger international effort to address damages caused by a Russian drone attack on the structure in February 2025. Ukraine’s Energy Minister, Denys Shmyhal, reported that the total estimated cost for restoring the shelter is approximately 500 million euros, or about $585 million. Discussions about financing the repairs took place at a recent international conference focused on the Chernobyl site. The U.S. contribution represents a significant step in ensuring the structural integrity of the containment dome, which remains a critical element in mitigating long-term radiation risks. International cooperation is expected to c

item.title: Thousands of Cancer Patients to Gain Access to a Quick NHS Injection
item.description: A new NHS jab that can be administered in just one minute is set to benefit tens of thousands of cancer patients. The jab is designed to help treat or manage specific forms of cancer more efficiently, providing patients with quicker relief and enhancing their treatment experience. This innovation represents a significant step forward in the fight against cancer, particularly for those who may have been experiencing delays or difficulties in receiving traditional therapies. The quick, one-minute jab has been hailed as a breakthrough that could reduce the burden on hospitals and healthcare providers while offering an effective solution for cancer patients. It is expected to become widely available across the NHS, potentially making a huge difference for patients in need of fast and reliable treatment options. The NHS aims to integrate this jab into routine cancer care, improving overall treatment outcomes and streamlining the process for cancer patients who require immediate or ongoing m

item.title: Fan Milk PLC Posts Strong First-Quarter Financial Performance in 2026
item.description: Fan Milk PLC began the 2026 financial year with a robust performance, reporting a profit before tax of GH₵61.2 million for the first quarter ended March 31, 2026, marking an 84% increase compared to GH₵33.2 million in the same period last year. Revenue grew by 33% to GH₵321.6 million, driven by steady consumer demand and resilient distribution networks despite economic pressures. Gross profit rose significantly from GH₵90.7 million to GH₵150.3 million, reflecting improved cost management, although the cost of sales also increased. Operating expenses, including sales, distribution, and administrative costs, saw notable rises, while finance costs nearly doubled due to higher interest obligations. Income tax expense surged to GH₵32 million, reducing the profit after tax to GH₵27.6 million, a 14.6% increase from the prior year. Earnings per share improved from GH₵0.21 to GH₵0.24. The company invested GH₵3.4 million in capital expenditure and retained earnings instead of paying dividends. C

item.title: Artist KC Green accuses AI startup Artisan of unauthorized use of his ‘This is fine’ comic in advertising
item.description:
Cartoonist KC Green, best known for creating the widely recognized “This is fine” comic, has accused AI startup Artisan of using his artwork without permission in a recent advertising campaign. The original comic, first published in 2013 as part of Green’s webcomic “Gunshow,” features a dog calmly sitting in a burning room and has since become a long-lasting internet meme. According to posts circulating on social media, Artisan allegedly modified the image for a subway advertisement, changing the caption to reference a failing business pipeline while promoting its AI sales tool, “Ava the AI BDR.”
Green publicly stated that he did not authorize the use of his work and described the situation as theft, comparing it to broader concerns about how AI companies use creative content. He also expressed frustration that he may need to pursue legal action, noting that doing so would take time away from his creative work. Artisan responded by saying it respects Green and has reached out to him,

item.title: The Truth Behind Intermittent Fasting: Benefits, Risks, and Who It Works For
item.description: Intermittent fasting (IF) has gained popularity as a health trend, but its effectiveness varies from person to person. Unlike traditional diets that focus on what you eat, IF is all about when you eat. By alternating between eating and fasting windows, it encourages the body to shift from burning glucose to burning fat for energy. Although IF can lead to weight loss and improve metabolic health, it is not a miracle solution or superior to other dietary strategies. The 16:8 method, 5:2 diet, and alternate-day fasting are common approaches, but they are not without their challenges, including hunger, irritability, and the risk of overeating during eating windows. While some studies suggest benefits for weight loss, diabetes management, and cardiovascular health, the long-term effects are still unclear. Furthermore, IF is not suitable for everyone—pregnant women, children, and those with eating disorders should avoid it. Ultimately, IF is just one tool in a broader healthy lifestyle that

item.title: Ed Sheeran joins Ipswich Town team to celebrate promotion to Premier League
